skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem:
The table below shows the distribution of ages, in years, of 20 participants in a community workshop.

AgeFrequency
301
311
322
333
345
358

Which of the following gives the correct order of the mean, median, and mode of the ages?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. The mode is the value with the highest frequency: 35. The median is the average of the 10th and 11th values when ordered; both are 34, so the median is 34. The mean is (30+31+32+32+33+33+33+34+34+34+34+34+35+35+35+35+35+35+35+35)/20 = 674/20 = 33.7. So mean (33.7) < median (34) < mode (35).

The other choices misorder these three measures.
